goal_primary: URL shortener — a small REST backend that turns a long URL into a generated short code and redirects visitors of that code to the original URL; tracks how many times each code is visited; supports deleting a code and rejecting invalid URLs; in-memory storage (no external DB).
owner_intent: I want a URL shortener: a small REST backend that takes a long URL and gives back a short code. When someone visits the short code, the service redirects them to the original long URL. Visiting an unknown code returns a not-found error. There is a stats endpoint that reports how many times a given code has been visited. A code can be deleted. If the submitted URL is missing or invalid, the request is rejected with a clear error. Every successful resolve increments that code's visit count. Keep storage simple and in-memory — no external database.
features: Shorten a long URL: submit a URL and get back a server-generated short code plus the short link
Resolve a short code: visiting the code redirects the client to the original long URL
Unknown short codes return a not-found response
Stats: look up how many times a given short code has been visited
Delete a short code so it no longer resolves
Validation: a missing or invalid URL is rejected with a clear error
The visit count for a code increments on each successful resolve
